translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The fear of a king is as the roaring of a lion: whoso provoketh him to anger sinneth against his own soul.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: It is an honour for a man to cease from strife: but every fool will be meddling.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The sluggard will not plow by reason of the cold; therefore shall he beg in harvest, and have nothing.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Counsel in the heart of man is like deep water; but a man of understanding will draw it out.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Most men will proclaim every one his own goodness: but a faithful man who can find?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The just man walketh in his integrity: his children are blessed after him.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: A king that sitteth in the throne of judgment scattereth away all evil with his eyes.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Who can say, I have made my heart clean, I am pure from my sin?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Divers weights, and divers measures, both of them are alike abomination to the LORD.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Even a child is known by his doings, whether his work be pure, and whether it be right.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The hearing ear, and the seeing eye, the LORD hath made even both of them.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Love not sleep, lest thou come to poverty; open thine eyes, and thou shalt be satisfied with bread.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: It is naught, it is naught, saith the buyer: but when he is gone his way, then he boasteth.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: There is gold, and a multitude of rubies: but the lips of knowledge are a precious jewel.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Take his garment that is surety for a stranger: and take a pledge of him for a strange woman.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Bread of deceit is sweet to a man; but afterwards his mouth shall be filled with gravel.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Every purpose is established by counsel: and with good advice make war.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He that goeth about as a talebearer revealeth secrets: therefore meddle not with him that flattereth with his lips.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Whoso curseth his father or his mother, his lamp shall be put out in obscure darkness.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: An inheritance may be gotten hastily at the beginning; but the end thereof shall not be blessed.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Say not thou, I will recompense evil; but wait on the LORD, and he shall save thee.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Divers weights are an abomination unto the LORD; and a false balance is not good.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Man’s goings are of the LORD; how can a man then understand his own way?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: It is a snare to the man who devoureth that which is holy, and after vows to make enquiry.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: A wise king scattereth the wicked, and bringeth the wheel over them.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The spirit of man is the candle of the LORD, searching all the inward parts of the belly.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Mercy and truth preserve the king: and his throne is upholden by mercy.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The glory of young men is their strength: and the beauty of old men is the grey head.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The blueness of a wound cleanseth away evil: so do stripes the inward parts of the belly.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The king’s heart is in the hand of the LORD, as the rivers of water: he turneth it whithersoever he will.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Every way of a man is right in his own eyes: but the LORD pondereth the hearts.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: To do justice and judgment is more acceptable to the LORD than sacrifice.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: An high look, and a proud heart, and the plowing of the wicked, is sin.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The thoughts of the diligent tend only to plenteousness; but of every one that is hasty only to want.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The getting of treasures by a lying tongue is a vanity tossed to and fro of them that seek death.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The robbery of the wicked shall destroy them; because they refuse to do judgment.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The way of man is froward and strange: but as for the pure, his work is right.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: It is better to dwell in a corner of the housetop, than with a brawling woman in a wide house.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The soul of the wicked desireth evil: his neighbour findeth no favour in his eyes.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: When the scorner is punished, the simple is made wise: and when the wise is instructed, he receiveth knowledge.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The righteous man wisely considereth the house of the wicked: but God overthroweth the wicked for their wickedness.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Whoso stoppeth his ears at the cry of the poor, he also shall cry himself, but shall not be heard.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: A gift in secret pacifieth anger: and a reward in the bosom strong wrath.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: It is joy to the just to do judgment: but destruction shall be to the workers of iniquity.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The man that wandereth out of the way of understanding shall remain in the congregation of the dead.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He that loveth pleasure shall be a poor man: he that loveth wine and oil shall not be rich.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The wicked shall be a ransom for the righteous, and the transgressor for the upright.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: It is better to dwell in the wilderness, than with a contentious and an angry woman.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: There is treasure to be desired and oil in the dwelling of the wise; but a foolish man spendeth it up.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He that followeth after righteousness and mercy findeth life, righteousness, and honour.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: A wise man scaleth the city of the mighty, and casteth down the strength of the confidence thereof.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Whoso keepeth his mouth and his tongue keepeth his soul from troubles.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Proud and haughty scorner is his name, who dealeth in proud wrath.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The desire of the slothful killeth him; for his hands refuse to labour.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He coveteth greedily all the day long: but the righteous giveth and spareth not.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The sacrifice of the wicked is abomination: how much more, when he bringeth it with a wicked mind?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: A false witness shall perish: but the man that heareth speaketh constantly.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: A wicked man hardeneth his face: but as for the upright, he directeth his way.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: There is no wisdom nor understanding nor counsel against the LORD.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The horse is prepared against the day of battle: but safety is of the LORD.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: A GOOD name is rather to be chosen than great riches, and loving favour rather than silver and gold.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The rich and poor meet together: the LORD is the maker of them all.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: A prudent man foreseeth the evil, and hideth himself: but the simple pass on, and are punished.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: By humility and the fear of the LORD are riches, and honour, and life.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Thorns and snares are in the way of the froward: he that doth keep his soul shall be far from them.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Train up a child in the way he should go: and when he is old, he will not depart from it.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The rich ruleth over the poor, and the borrower is servant to the lender.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He that soweth iniquity shall reap vanity: and the rod of his anger shall fail.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He that hath a bountiful eye shall be blessed; for he giveth of his bread to the poor.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Cast out the scorner, and contention shall go out; yea, strife and reproach shall cease.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He that loveth pureness of heart, for the grace of his lips the king shall be his friend.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The eyes of the LORD preserve knowledge, and he overthroweth the words of the transgressor.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The slothful man saith, There is a lion without, I shall be slain in the streets.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The mouth of strange women is a deep pit: he that is abhorred of the LORD shall fall therein.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Foolishness is bound in the heart of a child; but the rod of correction shall drive it far from him.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He that oppresseth the poor to increase his riches, and he that giveth to the rich, shall surely come to want.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Bow down thine ear, and hear the words of the wise, and apply thine heart unto my knowledge.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For it is a pleasant thing if thou keep them within thee; they shall withal be fitted in thy lips.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: That thy trust may be in the LORD, I have made known to thee this day, even to thee.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Have not I written to thee excellent things in counsels and knowledge,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: That I might make thee know the certainty of the words of truth; that thou mightest answer the words of truth to them that send unto thee?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Rob not the poor, because he is poor: neither oppress the afflicted in the gate:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For the LORD will plead their cause, and spoil the soul of those that spoiled them.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Make no friendship with an angry man; and with a furious man thou shalt not go:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Lest thou learn his ways, and get a snare to thy soul.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Be not thou one of them that strike hands, or of them that are sureties for debts.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: If thou hast nothing to pay, why should he take away thy bed from under thee?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Remove not the ancient landmark, which thy fathers have set.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Seest thou a man diligent in his business? he shall stand before kings; he shall not stand before mean men.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: When thou sittest to eat with a ruler, consider diligently what is before thee:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And put a knife to thy throat, if thou be a man given to appetite.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Be not desirous of his dainties: for they are deceitful meat.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Labour not to be rich: cease from thine own wisdom.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Wilt thou set thine eyes upon that which is not? for riches certainly make themselves wings; they fly away as an eagle toward heaven.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Eat thou not the bread of him that hath an evil eye, neither desire thou his dainty meats:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For as he thinketh in his heart, so is he: Eat and drink, saith he to thee; but his heart is not with thee.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The morsel which thou hast eaten shalt thou vomit up, and lose thy sweet words.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Speak not in the ears of a fool: for he will despise the wisdom of thy words.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Remove not the old landmark; and enter not into the fields of the fatherless:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For their redeemer is mighty; he shall plead their cause with thee.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Apply thine heart unto instruction, and thine ears to the words of knowledge.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Withhold not correction from the child: for if thou beatest him with the rod, he shall not die.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Thou shalt beat him with the rod, and shalt deliver his soul from hell.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: My son, if thine heart be wise, my heart shall rejoice, even mine.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Yea, my reins shall rejoice, when thy lips speak right things.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Let not thine heart envy sinners: but be thou in the fear of the LORD all the day long.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For surely there is an end; and thine expectation shall not be cut off.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Hear thou, my son, and be wise, and guide thine heart in the way.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Be not among winebibbers; among riotous eaters of flesh:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For the drunkard and the glutton shall come to poverty: and drowsiness shall clothe a man with rags.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Hearken unto thy father that begat thee, and despise not thy mother when she is old.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Buy the truth, and sell it not; also wisdom, and instruction, and understanding.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The father of the righteous shall greatly rejoice: and he that begetteth a wise child shall have joy of him.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Thy father and thy mother shall be glad, and she that bare thee shall rejoice.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: My son, give me thine heart, and let thine eyes observe my ways.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For a whore is a deep ditch; and a strange woman is a narrow pit.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: She also lieth in wait as for a prey, and increaseth the transgressors among men.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Who hath woe? who hath sorrow? who hath contentions? who hath babbling? who hath wounds without cause? who hath redness of eyes?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: They that tarry long at the wine; they that go to seek mixed wine.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Look not thou upon the wine when it is red, when it giveth his colour in the cup, when it moveth itself aright.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: At the last it biteth like a serpent, and stingeth like an adder.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Thine eyes shall behold strange women, and thine heart shall utter perverse things.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Yea, thou shalt be as he that lieth down in the midst of the sea, or as he that lieth upon the top of a mast.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: They have stricken me, shalt thou say, and I was not sick; they have beaten me, and I felt it not: when shall I awake? I will seek it yet again.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Be not thou envious against evil men, neither desire to be with them.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For their heart studieth destruction, and their lips talk of mischief.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Through wisdom is an house builded; and by understanding it is established:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And by knowledge shall the chambers be filled with all precious and pleasant riches.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: A wise man is strong; yea, a man of knowledge increaseth strength.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For by wise counsel thou shalt make thy war: and in multitude of counsellors there is safety.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Wisdom is too high for a fool: he openeth not his mouth in the gate.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He that deviseth to do evil shall be called a mischievous person.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The thought of foolishness is sin: and the scorner is an abomination to men.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: If thou faint in the day of adversity, thy strength is small.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: If thou forbear to deliver them that are drawn unto death, and those that are ready to be slain;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: If thou sayest, Behold, we knew it not; doth not he that pondereth the heart consider it? and he that keepeth thy soul, doth not he know it? and shall not he render to every man according to his works?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: My son, eat thou honey, because it is good; and the honeycomb, which is sweet to thy taste:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: So shall the knowledge of wisdom be unto thy soul: when thou hast found it, then there shall be a reward, and thy expectation shall not be cut off.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Lay not wait, O wicked man, against the dwelling of the righteous; spoil not his resting place:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For a just man falleth seven times, and riseth up again: but the wicked shall fall into mischief.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Rejoice not when thine enemy falleth, and let not thine heart be glad when he stumbleth:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Lest the LORD see it, and it displease him, and he turn away his wrath from him.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Fret not thyself because of evil men, neither be thou envious at the wicked;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For there shall be no reward to the evil man; the candle of the wicked shall be put out.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: My son, fear thou the LORD and the king: and meddle not with them that are given to change:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For their calamity shall rise suddenly; and who knoweth the ruin of them both?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: These things also belong to the wise. It is not good to have respect of persons in judgment.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He that saith unto the wicked, Thou art righteous; him shall the people curse, nations shall abhor him:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: But to them that rebuke him shall be delight, and a good blessing shall come upon them.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Every man shall kiss his lips that giveth a right answer.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Prepare thy work without, and make it fit for thyself in the field; and afterwards build thine house.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Be not a witness against thy neighbour without cause; and deceive not with thy lips.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Say not, I will do so to him as he hath done to me: I will render to the man according to his work.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I went by the field of the slothful, and by the vineyard of the man void of understanding;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And, lo, it was all grown over with thorns, and nettles had covered the face thereof, and the stone wall thereof was broken down.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Then I saw, and considered it well: I looked upon it, and received instruction.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Yet a little sleep, a little slumber, a little folding of the hands to sleep:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: So shall thy poverty come as one that travelleth; and thy want as an armed man.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: These are also proverbs of Solomon, which the men of Hezekiah king of Judah copied out.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: It is the glory of God to conceal a thing: but the honour of kings is to search out a matter.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The heaven for height, and the earth for depth, and the heart of kings is unsearchable.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Take away the dross from the silver, and there shall come forth a vessel for the finer.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Take away the wicked from before the king, and his throne shall be established in righteousness.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Put not forth thyself in the presence of the king, and stand not in the place of great men:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For better it is that it be said unto thee, Come up hither; than that thou shouldest be put lower in the presence of the prince whom thine eyes have seen.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Go not forth hastily to strive, lest thou know not what to do in the end thereof, when thy neighbour hath put thee to shame.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Debate thy cause with thy neighbour himself; and discover not a secret to another:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Lest he that heareth it put thee to shame, and thine infamy turn not away.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: A word fitly spoken is like apples of gold in pictures of silver.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: As an earring of gold, and an ornament of fine gold, so is a wise reprover upon an obedient ear.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: As the cold of snow in the time of harvest, so is a faithful messenger to them that send him: for he refresheth the soul of his masters.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Whoso boasteth himself of a false gift is like clouds and wind without rain.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: By long forbearing is a prince persuaded, and a soft tongue breaketh the bone.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Hast thou found honey? eat so much as is sufficient for thee, lest thou be filled therewith, and vomit it.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Withdraw thy foot from thy neighbour’s house; lest he be weary of thee, and so hate thee.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: A man that beareth false witness against his neighbour is a maul, and a sword, and a sharp arrow.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Confidence in an unfaithful man in time of trouble is like a broken tooth, and a foot out of joint.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: As he that taketh away a garment in cold weather, and as vinegar upon nitre, so is he that singeth songs to an heavy heart.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: If thine enemy be hungry, give him bread to eat; and if he be thirsty, give him water to drink:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For thou shalt heap coals of fire upon his head, and the LORD shall reward thee.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The north wind driveth away rain: so doth an angry countenance a backbiting tongue.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: It is better to dwell in the corner of the housetop, than with a brawling woman and in a wide house.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: As cold waters to a thirsty soul, so is good news from a far country.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: A righteous man falling down before the wicked is as a troubled fountain, and a corrupt spring.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: It is not good to eat much honey: so for men to search their own glory is not glory.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He that hath no rule over his own spirit is like a city that is broken down, and without walls.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: As snow in summer, and as rain in harvest, so honour is not seemly for a fool.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: As the bird by wandering, as the swallow by flying, so the curse causeless shall not come.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: A whip for the horse, a bridle for the ass, and a rod for the fool’s back.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Answer not a fool according to his folly, lest thou also be like unto him.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Answer a fool according to his folly, lest he be wise in his own conceit.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He that sendeth a message by the hand of a fool cutteth off the feet, and drinketh damage.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The legs of the lame are not equal: so is a parable in the mouth of fools.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: As he that bindeth a stone in a sling, so is he that giveth honour to a fool.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: As a thorn goeth up into the hand of a drunkard, so is a parable in the mouth of fools.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The great God that formed all things both rewardeth the fool, and rewardeth transgressors.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: As a dog returneth to his vomit, so a fool returneth to his folly.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Seest thou a man wise in his own conceit? there is more hope of a fool than of him.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The slothful man saith, There is a lion in the way; a lion is in the streets.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: As the door turneth upon his hinges, so doth the slothful upon his bed.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The slothful hideth his hand in his bosom; it grieveth him to bring it again to his mouth.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The sluggard is wiser in his own conceit than seven men that can render a reason.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He that passeth by, and meddleth with strife belonging not to him, is like one that taketh a dog by the ears.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: As a mad man who casteth firebrands, arrows, and death,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: So is the man that deceiveth his neighbour, and saith, Am not I in sport?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Where no wood is, there the fire goeth out: so where there is no talebearer, the strife ceaseth.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: As coals are to burning coals, and wood to fire; so is a contentious man to kindle strife.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The words of a talebearer are as wounds, and they go down into the innermost parts of the belly.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Burning lips and a wicked heart are like a potsherd covered with silver dross.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He that hateth dissembleth with his lips, and layeth up deceit within him;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: When he speaketh fair, believe him not: for there are seven abominations in his heart.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Whose hatred is covered by deceit, his wickedness shall be shewed before the whole congregation.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Whoso diggeth a pit shall fall therein: and he that rolleth a stone, it will return upon him.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: A lying tongue hateth those that are afflicted by it; and a flattering mouth worketh ruin.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Boast not thyself of to morrow; for thou knowest not what a day may bring forth.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Let another man praise thee, and not thine own mouth; a stranger, and not thine own lips.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: A stone is heavy, and the sand weighty; but a fool’s wrath is heavier than them both.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Wrath is cruel, and anger is outrageous; but who is able to stand before envy?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Open rebuke is better than secret love.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Faithful are the wounds of a friend; but the kisses of an enemy are deceitful.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The full soul loatheth an honeycomb; but to the hungry soul every bitter thing is sweet.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: As a bird that wandereth from her nest, so is a man that wandereth from his place.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Ointment and perfume rejoice the heart: so doth the sweetness of a man’s friend by hearty counsel.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Thine own friend, and thy father’s friend, forsake not; neither go into thy brother’s house in the day of thy calamity: for better is a neighbour that is near than a brother far off.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: My son, be wise, and make my heart glad, that I may answer him that reproacheth me.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: A prudent man foreseeth the evil, and hideth himself; but the simple pass on, and are punished.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Take his garment that is surety for a stranger, and take a pledge of him for a strange woman.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He that blesseth his friend with a loud voice, rising early in the morning, it shall be counted a curse to him.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: A continual dropping in a very rainy day and a contentious woman are alike.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Whosoever hideth her hideth the wind, and the ointment of his right hand, which bewrayeth itself.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Iron sharpeneth iron; so a man sharpeneth the countenance of his friend.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Whoso keepeth the fig tree shall eat the fruit thereof: so he that waiteth on his master shall be honoured.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: As in water face answereth to face, so the heart of man to man.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Hell and destruction are never full; so the eyes of man are never satisfied.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: As the fining pot for silver, and the furnace for gold; so is a man to his praise.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Though thou shouldest bray a fool in a mortar among wheat with a pestle, yet will not his foolishness depart from him.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Be thou diligent to know the state of thy flocks, and look well to thy herds.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For riches are not for ever: and doth the crown endure to every generation?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The hay appeareth, and the tender grass sheweth itself, and herbs of the mountains are gathered.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The lambs are for thy clothing, and the goats are the price of the field.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And thou shalt have goats’ milk enough for thy food, for the food of thy household, and for the maintenance for thy maidens.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The wicked flee when no man pursueth: but the righteous are bold as a lion.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For the transgression of a land many are the princes thereof: but by a man of understanding and knowledge the state thereof shall be prolonged.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: A poor man that oppresseth the poor is like a sweeping rain which leaveth no food.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: They that forsake the law praise the wicked: but such as keep the law contend with them.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Evil men understand not judgment: but they that seek the LORD understand all things.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Better is the poor that walketh in his uprightness, than he that is perverse in his ways, though he be rich.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Whoso keepeth the law is a wise son: but he that is a companion of riotous men shameth his father.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He that by usury and unjust gain increaseth his substance, he shall gather it for him that will pity the poor.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He that turneth away his ear from hearing the law, even his prayer shall be abomination.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Whoso causeth the righteous to go astray in an evil way, he shall fall himself into his own pit: but the upright shall have good things in possession.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The rich man is wise in his own conceit; but the poor that hath understanding searcheth him out.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: When righteous men do rejoice, there is great glory: but when the wicked rise, a man is hidden.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He that covereth his sins shall not prosper: but whoso confesseth and forsaketh them shall have mercy.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Happy is the man that feareth alway: but he that hardeneth his heart shall fall into mischief.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: As a roaring lion, and a ranging bear; so is a wicked ruler over the poor people.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The prince that wanteth understanding is also a great oppressor: but he that hateth covetousness shall prolong his days.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: A man that doeth violence to the blood of any person shall flee to the pit; let no man stay him.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Whoso walketh uprightly shall be saved: but he that is perverse in his ways shall fall at once.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He that tilleth his land shall have plenty of bread: but he that followeth after vain persons shall have poverty enough.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: A faithful man shall abound with blessings: but he that maketh haste to be rich shall not be innocent.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: To have respect of persons is not good: for for a piece of bread that man will transgress.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He that hasteth to be rich hath an evil eye, and considereth not that poverty shall come upon him.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He that rebuketh a man afterwards shall find more favour than he that flattereth with the tongue.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Whoso robbeth his father or his mother, and saith, It is no transgression; the same is the companion of a destroyer.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He that is of a proud heart stirreth up strife: but he that putteth his trust in the LORD shall be made fat.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He that trusteth in his own heart is a fool: but whoso walketh wisely, he shall be delivered.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He that giveth unto the poor shall not lack: but he that hideth his eyes shall have many a curse.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: When the wicked rise, men hide themselves: but when they perish, the righteous increase.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He, that being often reproved hardeneth his neck, shall suddenly be destroyed, and that without remedy.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: When the righteous are in authority, the people rejoice: but when the wicked beareth rule, the people mourn.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Whoso loveth wisdom rejoiceth his father: but he that keepeth company with harlots spendeth his substance.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The king by judgment establisheth the land: but he that receiveth gifts overthroweth it.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: A man that flattereth his neighbour spreadeth a net for his feet.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: In the transgression of an evil man there is a snare: but the righteous doth sing and rejoice.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The righteous considereth the cause of the poor: but the wicked regardeth not to know it.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Scornful men bring a city into a snare: but wise men turn away wrath.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: If a wise man contendeth with a foolish man, whether he rage or laugh, there is no rest.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The bloodthirsty hate the upright: but the just seek his soul.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: A fool uttereth all his mind: but a wise man keepeth it in till afterwards.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: If a ruler hearken to lies, all his servants are wicked.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The poor and the deceitful man meet together: the LORD lighteneth both their eyes.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The king that faithfully judgeth the poor, his throne shall be established for ever.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The rod and reproof give wisdom: but a child left to himself bringeth his mother to shame.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: When the wicked are multiplied, transgression increaseth: but the righteous shall see their fall.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Correct thy son, and he shall give thee rest; yea, he shall give delight unto thy soul.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Where there is no vision, the people perish: but he that keepeth the law, happy is he.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: A servant will not be corrected by words: for though he understand he will not answer.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Seest thou a man that is hasty in his words? there is more hope of a fool than of him.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He that delicately bringeth up his servant from a child shall have him become his son at the length.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: An angry man stirreth up strife, and a furious man aboundeth in transgression.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: A man’s pride shall bring him low: but honour shall uphold the humble in spirit.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Whoso is partner with a thief hateth his own soul: he heareth cursing, and bewrayeth it not.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The fear of man bringeth a snare: but whoso putteth his trust in the LORD shall be safe.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Many seek the ruler’s favour; but every man’s judgment cometh from the LORD.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: An unjust man is an abomination to the just: and he that is upright in the way is abomination to the wicked.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The words of Agur the son of Jakeh, even the prophecy: the man spake unto Ithiel, even unto Ithiel and Ucal,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Surely I am more brutish than any man, and have not the understanding of a man.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I neither learned wisdom, nor have the knowledge of the holy.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Who hath ascended up into heaven, or descended? who hath gathered the wind in his fists? who hath bound the waters in a garment? who hath established all the ends of the earth? what is his name, and what is his son’s name, if thou canst tell?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Every word of God is pure: he is a shield unto them that put their trust in him.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Add thou not unto his words, lest he reprove thee, and thou be found a liar.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Two things have I required of thee; deny me them not before I die:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Remove far from me vanity and lies: give me neither poverty nor riches; feed me with food convenient for me:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Lest I be full, and deny thee, and say, Who is the LORD? or lest I be poor, and steal, and take the name of my God in vain.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Accuse not a servant unto his master, lest he curse thee, and thou be found guilty.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: There is a generation that curseth their father, and doth not bless their mother.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: There is a generation that are pure in their own eyes, and yet is not washed from their filthiness.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: There is a generation, O how lofty are their eyes! and their eyelids are lifted up.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: There is a generation, whose teeth are as swords, and their jaw teeth as knives, to devour the poor from off the earth, and the needy from among men.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The horseleach hath two daughters, crying, Give, give. There are three things that are never satisfied, yea, four things say not, It is enough:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The grave; and the barren womb; the earth that is not filled with water; and the fire that saith not, It is enough.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The eye that mocketh at his father, and despiseth to obey his mother, the ravens of the valley shall pick it out, and the young eagles shall eat it.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: There be three things which are too wonderful for me, yea, four which I know not:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The way of an eagle in the air; the way of a serpent upon a rock; the way of a ship in the midst of the sea; and the way of a man with a maid.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Such is the way of an adulterous woman; she eateth, and wipeth her mouth, and saith, I have done no wickedness.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For three things the earth is disquieted, and for four which it cannot bear:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For a servant when he reigneth; and a fool when he is filled with meat;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For an odious woman when she is married; and an handmaid that is heir to her mistress.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: There be four things which are little upon the earth, but they are exceeding wise:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The ants are a people not strong, yet they prepare their meat in the summer;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The conies are but a feeble folk, yet make they their houses in the rocks;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The locusts have no king, yet go they forth all of them by bands;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The spider taketh hold with her hands, and is in kings’ palaces.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: There be three things which go well, yea, four are comely in going:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: A lion which is strongest among beasts, and turneth not away for any;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: A greyhound; an he goat also; and a king, against whom there is no rising up.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: If thou hast done foolishly in lifting up thyself, or if thou hast thought evil, lay thine hand upon thy mouth.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Surely the churning of milk bringeth forth butter, and the wringing of the nose bringeth forth blood: so the forcing of wrath bringeth forth strife.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The words of king Lemuel, the prophecy that his mother taught him.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: What, my son? and what, the son of my womb? and what, the son of my vows?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Give not thy strength unto women, nor thy ways to that which destroyeth kings.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: It is not for kings, O Lemuel, it is not for kings to drink wine; nor for princes strong drink:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Lest they drink, and forget the law, and pervert the judgment of any of the afflicted.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Give strong drink unto him that is ready to perish, and wine unto those that be of heavy hearts.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Let him drink, and forget his poverty, and remember his misery no more.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Open thy mouth for the dumb in the cause of all such as are appointed to destruction.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Open thy mouth, judge righteously, and plead the cause of the poor and needy.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Who can find a virtuous woman? for her price is far above rubies.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The heart of her husband doth safely trust in her, so that he shall have no need of spoil.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: She will do him good and not evil all the days of her life.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: She seeketh wool, and flax, and worketh willingly with her hands.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: She is like the merchants’ ships; she bringeth her food from afar.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: She riseth also while it is yet night, and giveth meat to her household, and a portion to her maidens.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: She considereth a field, and buyeth it: with the fruit of her hands she planteth a vineyard.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: She girdeth her loins with strength, and strengtheneth her arms.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: She perceiveth that her merchandise is good: her candle goeth not out by night.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: She layeth her hands to the spindle, and her hands hold the distaff.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: She stretcheth out her hand to the poor; yea, she reacheth forth her hands to the needy.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: She is not afraid of the snow for her household: for all her household are clothed with scarlet.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: She maketh herself coverings of tapestry; her clothing is silk and purple.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Her husband is known in the gates, when he sitteth among the elders of the land.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: She maketh fine linen, and selleth it; and delivereth girdles unto the merchant.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Strength and honour are her clothing; and she shall rejoice in time to come.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: She openeth her mouth with wisdom; and in her tongue is the law of kindness.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: She looketh well to the ways of her household, and eateth not the bread of idleness.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Her children arise up, and call her blessed; her husband also, and he praiseth her.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Many daughters have done virtuously, but thou excellest them all.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Favour is deceitful, and beauty is vain: but a woman that feareth the LORD, she shall be praised.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Give her of the fruit of her hands; and let her own works praise her in the gates.
